title: Mivoca Writing Analysis
description: Output of the NLP analysis pipeline for a single writing sample. Contains comprehensive lexical, syntactic, pragmatic, discourse, and formatting features extracted from the participant's text, along with readability scores and LIWC-equivalent psycholinguistic markers.

sample_id.description: Unique identifier for this writing sample analysis.

session_id.description: Identifier of the interview session that collected this writing sample.

prompt_id.description: Identifier of the prompt that elicited this writing sample.

prompt_type.description: Category of the writing prompt, determining the expected register and style context.

raw_text.description: The participant's original unmodified writing sample text.

word_count.description: Total number of words in the writing sample.

sentence_count.description: Total number of sentences in the writing sample.

lexical_features.description: Vocabulary richness and lexical diversity metrics.

mtld.description: Measure of Textual Lexical Diversity. Higher values indicate greater lexical diversity. Robust to text length variation.

mattr_window_50.description: Moving Average Type-Token Ratio with a 50-word window. Normalized lexical diversity measure between 0 and 1.

hapax_ratio.description: Proportion of words that appear exactly once (hapax legomena) in the sample.

avg_word_length_chars.description: Average word length measured in characters.

avg_word_length_syllables.description: Average word length measured in syllables.

latinate_germanic_ratio.description: Ratio of Latinate-origin to Germanic-origin words. Higher values indicate more formal, academic vocabulary.

vocabulary_sophistication.description: Proportion of low-frequency words based on corpus frequency norms. Higher values indicate more sophisticated vocabulary.

syntactic_features.description: Sentence structure and grammatical complexity metrics.

mean_sentence_length.description: Average number of words per sentence.

sentence_length_sd.description: Standard deviation of sentence lengths. Higher values indicate more varied sentence structure.

mean_clause_length.description: Average number of words per clause.

clauses_per_sentence.description: Average number of clauses per sentence. Indicates syntactic embedding depth.

dependent_clause_ratio.description: Proportion of clauses that are dependent (subordinate). Higher values indicate more complex subordination.

complex_t_unit_ratio.description: Proportion of T-units containing dependent clauses.

complex_nominal_per_t_unit.description: Average number of complex nominals (noun phrases with modifiers) per T-unit.

active_voice_ratio.description: Proportion of clauses in active voice (vs. passive). Higher values indicate more direct, active writing.

left_branching_ratio.description: Proportion of sentences with left-branching (front-loaded modifier) structure.

pragmatic_features.description: Interpersonal and rhetorical stance markers reflecting the writer's pragmatic choices.

hedge_count.description: Number of hedging expressions (e.g., 'perhaps', 'might', 'somewhat') indicating epistemic caution.

boost_count.description: Number of boosting expressions (e.g., 'clearly', 'obviously', 'certainly') indicating epistemic confidence.

hedge_boost_ratio.description: Ratio of hedges to boosters. Values > 1 indicate cautious stance; < 1 indicate confident stance.

self_mention_count.description: Count of first-person references indicating authorial presence.

engagement_markers.description: Count of reader-engagement devices (questions, directives, shared knowledge references).

attitude_markers.description: Count of explicit attitude expressions (evaluative adjectives, agreement/disagreement markers).

liwc_equivalent.description: Psycholinguistic markers equivalent to LIWC (Linguistic Inquiry and Word Count) summary dimensions.

analytical_thinking.description: Degree of formal, logical, and hierarchical thinking patterns (0-100).

clout.description: Relative social status and confidence expressed through language (0-100).

authenticity.description: Degree of honest, personal, and disclosing language (0-100).

emotional_tone.description: Overall emotional tone from negative (0) to positive (100). 50 = neutral.

first_person_singular_pct.description: Percentage of words that are first-person singular pronouns (I, me, my).

first_person_plural_pct.description: Percentage of words that are first-person plural pronouns (we, us, our).

second_person_pct.description: Percentage of words that are second-person pronouns (you, your).

positive_emotion_pct.description: Percentage of words conveying positive emotions.

negative_emotion_pct.description: Percentage of words conveying negative emotions.

cognitive_process_pct.description: Percentage of words related to cognitive processes (cause, know, think).

formatting_features.description: Punctuation and formatting choices that reveal stylistic preferences.

semicolons_per_100_words.description: Frequency of semicolons normalized per 100 words.

em_dashes_per_100_words.description: Frequency of em dashes normalized per 100 words.

exclamation_marks.description: Total count of exclamation marks in the sample.

question_marks.description: Total count of question marks in the sample.

parenthetical_asides.description: Count of parenthetical expressions (text within parentheses).

contractions_per_100_words.description: Frequency of contractions normalized per 100 words. Higher values suggest informal register.

avg_paragraph_length_words.description: Average number of words per paragraph.

discourse_features.description: Text cohesion and discourse-level organization metrics.

causal_connective_density.description: Density of causal connectives (because, therefore, so) per 100 words.

additive_connective_density.description: Density of additive connectives (and, also, moreover) per 100 words.

adversative_connective_density.description: Density of adversative connectives (but, however, although) per 100 words.

temporal_connective_density.description: Density of temporal connectives (then, before, after) per 100 words.

referential_cohesion.description: Degree of referential overlap between adjacent sentences (0 = no overlap, 1 = complete overlap).

propositional_idea_density.description: Average number of propositions (idea units) per sentence.

formality_f_score.description: Heylighen & Dewaele F-score measuring formality from contextual vs. non-contextual word classes. Range typically -100 to 100.

flesch_kincaid_grade.description: Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level indicating the U.S. school grade level needed to understand the text.

flesch_reading_ease.description: Flesch Reading Ease score. Higher values (up to ~120) indicate easier text; lower values indicate more difficult text.

gunning_fog.description: Gunning Fog Index estimating years of formal education needed to understand the text on first reading.
